title: PermissionDef
description:
A single catalog policy record, parsed strictly.

One `PermissionDef` is one *layer's* statement about an id. The cross-layer merge (see [`resolve_leaf`]) combines several of these for the same id.

category.description: Category tag (used by `include_categories` in slice 2; inert here).

file.description: File-access grants this permission carries (`[[file]]` sub-tables). Each is parsed strictly and its path validated at the read boundary.

id.description: Permission id (top-level in slice 1; namespaced ids are slice 2).

include_categories.description: Categories this permission aggregates. Inert in slice 1.

includes.description: Explicit ids this permission aggregates. Inert in slice 1.

limits.description: Resource limits this permission sets. A higher layer that sets `limits` replaces wholesale (limits are a small fixed struct, not a list). Uses the strict [`CatalogLimits`] so an unknown key (notably `mac_mask`) under `[limits]` is rejected rather than silently dropped.

replace.description: When `true`, this layer wipes everything accumulated from lower layers and takes its own statement wholesale (full override, not field-by-field).

risk.description: Risk class. Optional per-layer: a higher layer may override primitives without restating risk, in which case the lower layer's risk stands.

Access.description:
File-access mode a grant requests.

`ro` maps to POSIX ACL `r-X` (read + traverse-only on directories — the `X` is execute *only* on dirs, so a reader can walk into a tree without gaining execute on regular files); `rw` maps to `rwX`. The two values form an ordered lattice for the resolve-time union (`Ro` < `Rw`): two grants on the same path merge to the wider access.

CatalogLimits.description:
The `[limits]` sub-table as written in a catalog record, parsed *strictly*.

`rolestore::Limits` is intentionally tolerant (no `deny_unknown_fields`) because Tessera owns the role schema and Census must ignore role fields it does not consume. The catalog is the opposite: Census owns it and expands it as root, so an unknown key under `[limits]` is a typo or a smuggled field and must be rejected — most importantly `mac_mask`, which is a Tessera enforcement primitive and MUST NOT appear in a catalog expansion. Hiding it under `[limits]` (where the tolerant role type would silently drop it) would otherwise be worse than the correctly-rejected top-level form. This local strict type closes that gap without touching the tolerant role-slice parse.

FileGrant.description:
A single file-access grant as written in a catalog record, parsed *strictly*.

Strict (`deny_unknown_fields`) for the same reason the whole catalog is: Census owns this format and materializes it as root (setfacl). An unrecognised key is a typo or a smuggled field, not something to silently ignore.

The `path` is validated (absolute, no control chars, no `..` component) at the read boundary; `{param}` placeholders are filled and re-validated at resolve time, mirroring the parametrized-sudo path exactly.

path.description: Absolute path to a directory, file, or glob pattern.

recursive.description: For directories: apply recursively and set a default-ACL so new files in the tree inherit the access. Absent defaults to `false`.

Risk.description: Risk class of a permission. Advisory only (honest labelling, not enforcement): it never blocks expansion or apply.

oneOf.description: Capability stays within its intended scope.
Capability can be a path to root (e.g. membership in `docker`).
